evidence: "For Bergman devotees like me, any morsel tossed our way by the great master is a treat."
"It’s a devastating 'portrait of the artist as a young man.'"
"The film is comprised of focused sequences, punctuated by close-ups of each of the four women that dissolve into blood red as the 'cries and whispers' of their psyches haunt the soundtrack."
"This is an unrelentingly bleak and slow 2 1/2-hour film."
"The pacing and eventual horror creep up on you slowly."
"All of the performances are outstanding, as is the design, cinematography and script."
"It's much better than I expected. No, it's not Criterion Collection quality, but it's OK."
"For Bergman devotees like me, any morsel tossed our way by the great master is a treat."
"Bergman is reacting to many things here--including his and his nation's own mixed feelings of neutrality and complicity during WWII."
"Scenes of horror and emotional brutality are then punctuated by moments of incredible beauty, humanity and redemption."
"The film's themes of retrospection, regret, loss and becoming reconciled touched me deeply."
"You leave this film feeling you've been taken somewhere you've never been before."
"The concluding scene... always brings me to tears."

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex, slow-paced dramas, particularly those by Ingmar Bergman. They value high production quality and emotional depth, often seeking films that evoke strong feelings and introspection.
